texto: The gaze of others is quite without indulgence for our defects and that of Mantegna is pitiless. I am grateful to him. Harshness, in the realm of the arts, is a virtue, and it is sometimes a good thing to see oneself as one is. My stupor, however, comes from the fact that people recognise me where I myself seem to see a stranger. This leads one to meditate more deeply on the matter. Are they dwelling on my superficial appearance rather than on what I really am? Who can say?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Marie Ferranti que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “The gaze of others is quite without indulgence for our defects and that of Mantegna is pitiless. …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre gratidao, appearance, identity, com foco em identity.
